rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they refer to visible ports and features.
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model to define assumptions and guarantees; avoid implementation-specific subcomponent paths in type contracts.
generic_rules: Declare constants and equations in AGREE annexes only when they are explicitly named or bounded in the requirement.
Ensure that all AGREE expressions use types that match the AADL model's port and data definitions.
Write guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs without referencing internal implementation details.
Avoid encoding AADL properties like timing or resource constraints directly in AGREE expressions unless exposed as typed variables.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference valid component features and ports from the AADL model.
Boolean expressions in AGREE must align with the actual data types of the referenced ports or variables.
Use `eq` declarations for helper variables that define ranges or conditions in AGREE contracts.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports or internal implementation paths in component type contracts.
Do not attempt to encode AADL timing or resource properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and their mapping to AGREE expressions
Implementation-level bindings and assignments in component implementations
